title: lupinus lepidus subsp. cusickii
intro: Lupinus lepidus subsp. cusickii, also known as Cusick's lupine, is a subspecies of lupine that is native to the mountainous regions of North America. It is a perennial herb with a rich history of traditional use, primarily valued for its medicinal and nutritional properties. The plant is rich in protein, fiber, and isoflavones, which have been shown to have potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, and may also help to lower cholesterol levels and improve cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, Lupinus lepidus subsp. cusickii is often used as a dietary supplement, and can be consumed as a tea, capsule, or powder, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
